channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: European Union finalizes trade deal with U.S. setting tariff limits on key exports
item.description: The European Union has approved a trade agreement with the United States that caps tariffs on most EU exports at 15 per cent, avoiding a direct confrontation with President Donald Trump ahead of his J...

item.title: Raja’s Appam: A Small Taman Melawati Eatery Serving Fluffy, Flavorful Appams
item.description: Located in Taman Melawati, Raja’s Appam has become a beloved local spot for traditional South Indian-style pancakes called appam. Originally operating as a catering service from 2017 to 2020, the busi...

item.title: Prince Rahim Aga Khan V visits Islamabad, to meet officials from Gilgit-Baltistan
item.description: Prince Rahim Aga Khan V, who is the spiritual leader of the Ismaili community, arrived in Islamabad on Wednesday, as confirmed by the Presidency. He was warmly received at Nur Khan Airbase by Presiden...

item.title: Sen. John Cornyn briefly shares and deletes post criticizing Trump ahead of Texas Senate runoff
item.description: Senator John Cornyn (R-TX) briefly shared a social media post labeling former President Donald Trump the 'Commander-in-Cheat' before quickly deleting it. The post, originating from the advocacy group ...

item.title: Princess Nandi Zulu to run Cape Town Marathon to support children's charities
item.description: Princess Nandi Zulu, daughter of the late King Zwelithini kaBhekuzulu, will be running the 42km Sanlam Cape Town Marathon this Sunday to raise funds for various children's charities, including Bobbie ...

item.title: Teenager arrested after man in his 60s stabbed at Stafford golf course
item.description: A 17-year-old boy from Stafford has been arrested on suspicion of attempted murder following an attack on a man in his 60s at Castle Golf Club. The incident occurred on the evening of 14 May when the ...

item.title: Daily Life and Culture in Japan During the 1920s
item.description: The 1920s in Japan, often referred to as the era of Taisho Democracy, was a period of significant cultural and social transformation. Cities like Tokyo and Osaka experienced rapid Westernization, refl...

item.title: Fashionable Gentlemen and Their Groomed Dog in 1880s Stockholm
item.description: In 1880s Stockholm, two gentlemen walked the streets dressed in the era's finest fashion. Their top hats, tailored coats, and polished boots reflected both their social standing and the importance of ...

item.title: Mao Aplasca surrenders firearm following Senate gunfire incident, claims self-defense
item.description: Former Senate security personnel Mao Aplasca surrendered his firearm to authorities after being linked to a gunfire incident inside the Philippine Senate. Aplasca maintained that the Senate was under ...

item.title: Civil Aviation Minister urges preparedness amid West Asia tensions, assures no immediate risk to India
item.description: Union Civil Aviation Minister Rammohan Naidu Kinjarapu on May 20, 2026, during his maiden visit to Ranchi, emphasised that India should remain alert to any potential consequences arising from the esca...

item.title: Southampton challenges expulsion from Championship play-off over spying incident
item.description: Southampton don drag English Football League to appeal after dem comot dem from the Championship play-off final. The club talk say the punishment wey dem give dem na too serious compared to wetin don ...

item.title: Google Introduces AI Agents to Provide Continuous, Personalized Search Updates
item.description: At Google I/O 2026, Google unveiled a major evolution in Search with the introduction of agentic AI capabilities. These new AI agents allow users to create, customize, and manage multiple assistants t...

item.title: Barangay secretary and community watchman shot dead in Cotabato ambush
item.description: COTABATO CITY — A tragic ambush took place on Tuesday afternoon, May 19, in Barangay Kabasalan, Ligawasan town, Cotabato, resulting in the deaths of a barangay secretary and a volunteer community watc...

item.title: Polls Suggest Texas Senate Race Could Be Competitive for Democrats
item.description: Recent analysis by CNN analyst Harry Enten indicates that Democrats may have a genuine opportunity to win a Senate seat in Texas, a state long considered solidly Republican. This insight comes ahead o...

item.title: Kenya Power to Relocate 9 Transmission Towers Following Heavy Rain Damage at Lake Nakuru
item.description: Kenya Power has revealed plans to relocate nine transmission towers along Lake Nakuru after the recent heavy rains caused rising water levels, which threatened the Lessos–Lanet 132kV double circuit tr...

item.title: Kenyan Journalist Credits Former Teacher for Saving His Education and Supporting Him Through University
item.description: Leonard Kakaloo Onyango, a Kenyan journalist, recently shared a touching story about how his former high school teacher, Agnes Mulamba, lovingly called Becky, rescued him from dropping out of school. ...

item.title: Strategies for Sierra Leone to Ensure Mining Benefits Local Communities
item.description: Sierra Leone depends heavily on minerals, making almost 80% of its export revenue from mining. Despite this, many mining communities stay poor and often lose land because government decisions are made...

item.title: The Decline in International Student Enrolments in Australia
item.description: Australian universities have benefitted enormously from decades of international student enrolments, which have provided both economic and cultural advantages. The government first permitted public un...

item.title: Inside London Records: The Creative Freedom and Excess of a 90s Music Label
item.description: London Records, a label originally part of Decca Records and later independent under Polygram, became emblematic of 1990s British pop and dance music. Known for its edgy and diverse roster, including ...

item.title: ‘Elephants in the Fog’ Explores the Lives and Challenges of Nepal’s Kinnar Community
item.description: Abinash Bikram Shah’s feature debut, 'Elephants in the Fog,' offers an intimate and compelling portrayal of Nepal’s Kinnar community, legally recognized as part of the country's third gender. Set in a...
